problems.title: CUDA Learn 1: Program Structure and nvcc
problems.instructions:
CUDA programs are compiled with nvcc (the NVIDIA CUDA compiler). A CUDA program can look like normal C++ code.

In this problem, you will write a minimal CUDA program that:
1. Includes <cstdio>
2. Defines a main() function
3. Uses printf to print exactly:

CUDA programs are compiled with nvcc!

This problem introduces:
- #include syntax
- int main()
- printf
- return 0;

Type the correct syntax in the correct order.

tests.expected_stdout: CUDA programs are compiled with nvcc!

problems.title: CUDA Learn 2: Writing Your First __global__ Kernel
CUDA Learn 3: Launching a Kernel with <<< >>>
problems.instructions:
Kernels are launched using special syntax:

kernelName<<<numBlocks, threadsPerBlock>>>();

In this problem:
1. Define a kernel called helloKernel that prints:
   Hello from thread!\n
2. In main(), launch the kernel with:
   1 block and 1 thread

3. Call cudaDeviceSynchronize() after launching the kernel.

You must include <cuda_runtime.h>.

problems.title: CUDA Learn 4: Using threadIdx
problems.instructions:
Each thread has a built-in variable called threadIdx.

threadIdx.x gives the thread's index inside its block.

In this problem:
1. Write a kernel that prints:
   Thread ID: X

2. Launch the kernel with 1 block and 4 threads.

Each thread should print its own threadIdx.x.

problems.title: CUDA Learn 5: blockIdx and blockDim
problems.instructions:
CUDA organizes threads into blocks and grids.

Built-in variables:
- blockIdx.x  (block index)
- blockDim.x  (threads per block)

Global thread index formula:
int globalId = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x;

In this problem:
1. Print the global thread ID
2. Launch 2 blocks with 3 threads each

Each thread must compute and print its global ID.

problems.title: CUDA Learn 6: Allocating Device Memory with cudaMalloc
problems.instructions:
To allocate memory on the GPU, use:

cudaMalloc((void**)&ptr, sizeInBytes);

In this problem:
1. Allocate space on the device for 5 integers
2. Free the memory using cudaFree

Include <cuda_runtime.h>.

You do NOT need to use the memory yet.

problems.title: CUDA Learn 7: Copying Memory with cudaMemcpy
CUDA Learn 8: Simple Parallel Addition
problems.instructions:
In this problem you will combine:
- __global__ kernels
- threadIdx.x
- cudaMalloc
- cudaMemcpy

Write a kernel that adds 1 to each element of an integer array.

Steps:
1. Host array: {5, 6, 7, 8}
2. Copy to device
3. Launch kernel with 1 block, 4 threads
4. Each thread adds 1 to its corresponding element
5. Copy result back and print values

Expected output:
6
7
8
9
